message.content: Account Manipulation: Additional Cloud Roles involves adversaries adding roles or permissions to compromised cloud accounts to maintain persistent access and escalate privileges. This technique can lead to extensive access to data and settings, including the ability to reset admin passwords. Common exploitation methods include updating IAM policies, adding global administrators in Office 365, and modifying existing valid accounts. In Azure AD, adversaries might use the Application Administrator role to gain additional permissions, while in AWS, they could use APIs like CreatePolicyVersion or AttachUserPolicy to alter IAM policies. Notable groups like APT29 and LAPSUS$ have used this technique to grant elevated privileges to compromised accounts. Mitigation strategies include enforcing the principle of least privilege, regularly auditing account roles and permissions, implementing multi-factor authentication, and monitoring for unusual account activity.
